name: shopify-apps
description: Expert patterns for Shopify app development including Remix/React Router apps, embedded apps with App Bridge, webhook handling, GraphQL Admin API, Polaris components, billing, and app extensions. Use when: shopify app, shopify, embedded app, polaris, app bridge.
capabilities: Generate Remix-based Shopify app boilerplate with proper routing structure
Implement App Bridge for embedded admin experiences
Create secure webhook handlers with HMAC signature verification
Build Polaris UI components following Shopify design guidelines
Construct GraphQL queries for Shopify Admin API operations
Set up billing integration with Shopify Subscription API
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analyzer flagged two false positives for weak cryptographic algorithms at SKILL.md lines 3 and 46. Both are documentation text, not executable code. Line 3 mentions HMAC verification as a best practice. Line 46 is descriptive text. This is a documentation skill with no code execution risk.
valueStatement: Shopify app development requires understanding complex integration patterns. This skill provides proven patterns for embedded apps, webhook handling, and GraphQL API usage.
userTitle: Build Shopify Apps with Modern Patterns
limitations: Does not execute code or make actual API calls to Shopify
Cannot test webhook endpoints or verify live integrations
Requires developer to implement actual authentication flows
Patterns assume Shopify CLI and Node.js runtime environment

useCases.targetUser: Full-stack developers building custom Shopify integrations
useCases.title: Shopify App Scaffold
useCases.description: Generate a complete Shopify app structure with Remix, App Bridge, and Polaris for rapid project initialization.

useCases.targetUser: Backend engineers integrating Shopify store events
useCases.title: Webhook Handler Implementation
useCases.description: Create secure webhook endpoints with proper HMAC verification and async processing patterns.

useCases.targetUser: API developers migrating from REST to GraphQL
useCases.title: GraphQL API Integration
useCases.description: Build efficient GraphQL queries and mutations for product, order, and customer management.

promptTemplates.title: Create Basic Shopify App Structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a Shopify app using Remix with App Bridge integration. Include the basic file structure, package.json dependencies, and main entry points for an embedded app.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new Shopify app project from scratch

promptTemplates.title: Implement Webhook Handler
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a webhook handler for the orders/create webhook. Include HMAC signature verification, async processing with a queue, and proper error handling for retry logic.

promptTemplates.title: Build Polaris Data Table
promptTemplates.description: Generate a Polaris data table component to display products with sorting, filtering, and bulk actions.
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Polaris IndexTable component for displaying Shopify products. Include column definitions for title, vendor, price, and inventory. Add sorting by created date and filtering by vendor.

promptTemplates.title: GraphQL Product Query
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a GraphQL query to fetch products with variants, including inventory levels across locations. Add pagination support with cursor-based navigation.
promptTemplates.scenario: Retrieving product data efficiently from Shopify Admin API

bestPractices: Use GraphQL Admin API for all new development instead of REST for better performance and flexibility
Process webhooks asynchronously and respond within 200ms to prevent Shopify timeouts
Request customer data access scopes only when required and handle GDPR deletion requests
antiPatterns: Using REST API for new app development when GraphQL provides better capabilities
Synchronous webhook processing that exceeds Shopify 200ms response timeout
Polling Shopify API for changes instead of using webhooks for real-time updates

faq.question: What is the recommended stack for new Shopify apps in 2024?
faq.answer: Use Remix with React Router for the backend, App Bridge v3 for embedded experiences, Polaris for UI components, and GraphQL Admin API for all data operations.

faq.question: How do I handle webhook timeouts from Shopify?
faq.answer: Respond with 200 status within 200ms, then process webhook data asynchronously using a job queue. Shopify retries failed webhooks with exponential backoff.

faq.question: When should I use App Bridge versus direct navigation?
faq.answer: Always use App Bridge for embedded apps in the Shopify Admin. It provides seamless navigation, modal support, and proper session handling within the Shopify iframe.

faq.question: How do I implement billing for my Shopify app?
faq.answer: Use the Shopify Billing API with GraphQL mutations. Create recurring charges or one-time purchases, handle the confirmation URL redirect, and verify charge status before granting access.

faq.question: What are the rate limits for Shopify Admin API?
faq.answer: Shopify uses a leaky bucket algorithm with query cost-based limiting. Monitor the X-Shopify-Shop-Domain and rate limit headers. GraphQL queries have calculated costs based on complexity.

faq.question: How do I handle GDPR compliance in my Shopify app?
faq.answer: Implement webhook handlers for customers/data_request, customers/redact, and shop/redact. Store customer data minimally and delete upon request within 30 days as required by Shopify.
